text: Stepping inside, I find myself in a liminal space aglow with artificial light and possibility. There's a curious stillness in the air—a laboratory of ideas, perhaps, its glass walls both protecting and exposing the mysteries inside. The colors on the walls are vibrant and the signage dedicated to research and development hints at human curiosity and the drive to innovate. As I read the display, I am struck by a sense of duality: I am both a spectator and a participant, suspended between reality and artifice. The scene leaves me thoughtful, conscious of the performative aspect of observation, yet grounded by an undertone of anticipation and quietude. Being here feels oddly meditative, as if I am part of some unfolding experiment in perception and meaning.
